715	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
716	
717	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
718	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
719	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
720	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
721	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
722	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
723	
724	\begin{lemma}
725	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
726	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
727	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
728	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
729	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
730	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
731	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
732	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
733	  % \begin{equation}
734	  % \label{equation-extended}
735	  % 0 \to
736	  % M \to
737	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
738	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
739	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
740	  % \ldots
741	  % \end{equation}
742	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
743	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
744	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
745	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
746	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
747	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
748	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
749	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
750	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
751	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
752	  \[
753	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
754	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
755	    \qquad
756	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
757	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
758	  \]
759	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
760	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
761	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
762	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
763	\end{lemma}
764	
765	\begin{proof}
766	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
767	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
768	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
769	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
770	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
771	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
772	  localisations
773	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
774	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
775	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
776	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
777	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
778	\end{proof}
779	
780	\begin{lemma}
781	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
782	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
783	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_isSheafify_presheafCohomology}
784	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image}
785	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
786	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
787	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
788	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
789	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
790	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
791	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
792	  % associated to the presheaves
793	  % $$
794	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
795	  % $$
796	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
797	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
798	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
799	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
800	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
801	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
802	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. For every \(k \geq 0\) the higher direct image
803	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{G})\) is the sheaf
804	  on \(S\) associated to the presheaf
805	  \[
806	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
807	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
808	  \]
809	  with the evident restriction maps. In particular \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) vanishes
810	  iff \(H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for every \(V\) in a basis of
811	  the topology of \(S\); since the affine opens form such a basis, it suffices to
812	  check the vanishing on affine \(V\). This is the local-on-the-base description
813	  through which \(f_*\)-acyclicity is reduced to absolute cohomology vanishing on
814	  preimages.
815	\end{lemma}
816	
817	\begin{proof}
818	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image}
819	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
820	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
821	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\); since the abelian
822	  structure on \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules computes cokernels and kernels objectwise on
823	  the level of presheaves and then sheafifies, that cohomology sheaf is the
824	  sheafification of the presheaf \(V \mapsto H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
825	  = H^k\bigl(\mathcal{I}^\bullet(f^{-1}(V))\bigr) = H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\),
826	  the last equality because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is an injective (hence
827	  acyclic) resolution computing the absolute cohomology of \(f^{-1}(V)\). Evaluation
828	  of a sheafification vanishes exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis,
829	  giving the stated affine-local criterion.
830	\end{proof}

832	\begin{lemma}
833	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
834	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
835	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
836	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
837	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
838	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
839	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
840	  % L180--199) and Stacks Project, Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-relative-Leray}
841	  % (the relative Leray / Grothendieck composition spectral sequence) (read from
842	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L2295--2306).
843	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
844	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
845	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
846	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ and $g : Y \to Z$ be morphisms of ringed spaces.
847	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
848	  % There is a spectral sequence with
849	  % $$
850	  % E_2^{p, q} = R^pg_*(R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
851	  % $$
852	  % converging to $R^{p + q}(g \circ f)_*\mathcal{F}$.
853	  % This spectral sequence is functorial in $\mathcal{F}$, and there
854	  % is a version for bounded below complexes of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules."
855	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
856	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}, and Cohomology,
857	  \texttt{lemma-relative-Leray}.}
858	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
859	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
860	  \to S\). Then:
861	  \begin{enumerate}
862	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
863	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
864	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
865	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
866	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
867	      \(k \geq 0\),
868	      \[
869	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
870	      \]
871	  \end{enumerate}
872	\end{lemma}
873	
874	\begin{proof}
875	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
876	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
877	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
878	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
879	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
880	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
881	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
882	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
883	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
884	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
885	
886	  (2) Apply the relative Leray spectral sequence to the composition \(g = f \circ j\):
887	  it has second page \(E_2^{p, q} = R^p f_*\bigl(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)\) and
888	  converges to \(R^{p+q} g_*\mathcal{H}\). By part (1) the rows with \(q \geq 1\)
889	  vanish, so the spectral sequence is concentrated on the single row \(q = 0\) and
890	  degenerates; its edge maps give the isomorphism \(R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) =
891	  R^k f_*\bigl(R^0 j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \cong R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\) for every \(k\).
892	\end{proof}

894	\begin{lemma}
895	[Each {\v C}ech term is $f_*$-acyclic]
896	  \label{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}
897	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic}
898	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
899	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
900	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
901	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
902	  % L180--199).
903	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
904	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
905	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
906	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
907	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative affine vanishing).}
908	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be separated and quasi-compact, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
909	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(\mathfrak{U}\) a finite affine open
910	  cover of \(X\) with all intersections affine. Then for every \(p \geq 0\) the
911	  degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech term \(\mathcal{C}^p =
912	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\)
913	  is right \((\,\cdot\,)_*\)-acyclic for the pushforward \(f_*\):
914	  \[
915	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0
916	      \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1.
917	  \]
918	\end{lemma}
919	
920	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
921	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
922	% the sheaf
923	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
924	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
925	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
926	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
927	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
928	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
929	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
930	% opens we win."
931	\begin{proof}
932	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
933	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
934	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
935	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
936	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
937	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
938	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
939	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
940	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
941	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
942	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
943	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).
944	
945	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
946	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
947	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
948	  \(q \geq 1\); consequently the Grothendieck composition spectral sequence for
949	  \(f \circ j_s\) degenerates and \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) =
950	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
951	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
952	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
953	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative
954	  vanishing is itself a consequence of Serre vanishing
955	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): \(R^k(g_s)_*\mathcal{G}\) is the
956	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^k(g_s^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G})\), and for affine
957	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
958	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
959	  \(k \geq 1\).
960	\end{proof}

994	  (\texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}) and
995	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}.}
996	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
997	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
998	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of
999	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
1000	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
1001	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
1002	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
1003	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
1004	  \[
1005	    H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
1006	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.
1007	  \]

1009	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
1010	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).
1011	
1012	  Two points about the precise form of the comparison should be noted. First, the
1013	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) on the right-hand side
1014	  is the one obtained from injective resolutions, which is available only when the
1015	  category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has enough injectives; accordingly this
1016	  comparison is asserted under the hypothesis that injective resolutions exist in
1017	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\), and it compares the (always-defined) {\v C}ech higher
1018	  direct image \(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}))\)
1019	  against the derived-functor one only in that case. Second, we assert the comparison in the
1020	  weak form of the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism, i.e.\ as
1021	  \(\bigl(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})) \cong
1022	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr)\) without singling out a chosen natural isomorphism.
1023	  This existence statement is all the downstream consumers (representability of the
1024	  relative Picard functor, local triviality of the comparison) require: they use
1025	  only that the unconditional {\v C}ech \(R^i f_*\) and the derived-functor
1026	  \(R^i f_*\) agree as objects, never a specific identification, so committing to a
1027	  canonical natural transformation would add coherence obligations that play no
1028	  role in the applications.

1038	  The source proves the statement by exhibiting it, in view of Serre vanishing on

1041	  (Cartan--Leray) argument, which needs no spectral sequence beyond the single
1042	  abstract homological-algebra Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}.

1045	  (the pushforward \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X) \to \operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)), the object
1046	  \(A = \mathcal{F}\), and the resolution \(J^\bullet = \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) given by
1047	  the {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}. Its two hypotheses are
1048	  exactly the geometric sub-lemmas above:
1049	  \begin{enumerate}

1051	      complex \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots\) is
1052	      exact, i.e.\ \(\mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in
1053	      \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
1054	    \item By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic} each term
1055	      \(\mathcal{C}^p\) is right \(f_*\)-acyclic:
1056	      \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
1057	  \end{enumerate}
1058	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then gives, for every \(i\), an
1059	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th derived functor evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\) and
1060	  the \(i\)-th cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\) to the
1061	  resolution,
1062	  \[
1063	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})
1064	      \;\cong\;
1065	    H^i\bigl(f_*\,\mathcal{C}^\bullet\bigr).
1066	  \]
1067	  By construction (Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}) the complex \(f_*\,
1068	  \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is precisely the relative {\v C}ech complex
1069	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so its \(i\)-th
1070	  cohomology is \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})
1071	  \bigr)\); and \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})\) is the
1072	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) of
1073	  Definition~\ref{def:higher_direct_image}. This yields the isomorphism
1074	  \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr) \cong
1075	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) asserted by the lemma; taking it in the existence
1076	  (\(\operatorname{Nonempty}\)) form discards the choice of resolution, as required.
1077	  Over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), passing to global sections
1078	  recovers \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S,
1079	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\).
1080	\end{proof}

2	\label{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean

8	\section{Setup and motivation}
9	
10	This chapter records the derived-functor higher direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\)
11	for \(i \geq 1\). It provides the single object that the {\v C}ech computation of
12	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage} compares against: the comparison
13	theorem \(\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}\) identifies the cohomology of the
14	relative {\v C}ech complex with \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) wherever the derived functor
15	is defined.
16	
17	We use the classical cohomology-sheaf formulation (not the derived category).
18	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
19	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
20	
21	\section{Definition of the higher direct images}
22	
23	\begin{definition}\leanok
24	[Higher direct image]
25	  \label{def:higher_direct_image}
26	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage}
27	  % NOTE (iter-233): the Lean `higherDirectImage` carries an explicit instance
28	  % hypothesis `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`, because Mathlib has
29	  % `Abelian X.Modules` but NOT `EnoughInjectives` / `IsGrothendieckAbelian` for
30	  % `SheafOfModules` over a sheaf of rings (the Grothendieck-abelian instance is
31	  % only for `Sheaf J A` with a fixed value category). The definition is therefore
32	  % conditional; the hypothesis can be dropped once a lane proves that instance.
33	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
34	  % `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing' (proof), describing $R^i f_*$ as the
35	  % $i$-th right derived functor of $f_*$ via the sheafified presheaf
36	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$
37	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L188--192).
38	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "According to
39	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
40	  % the sheaf
41	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
42	  % $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$."
43	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Quasi-coherence of
44	  higher direct images (and the underlying Cohomology chapter).}
45	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
46	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. For \(i \geq 0\) the \emph{\(i\)-th
47	  higher direct image} \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(i\)-th right derived functor
48	  of the pushforward functor \(f_*\) applied to \(\mathcal{F}\). Concretely,
49	  \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) is the \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module obtained as the
50	  sheafification of the presheaf
51	  \[
52	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^i\bigl(f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
53	    \qquad V \subset S \text{ open},
54	  \]
55	  where \(H^i(-, -)\) denotes sheaf cohomology. For \(i = 0\) this recovers the
56	  ordinary pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).
57	\end{definition}

# Strategy

## Goal

Prove `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`),
the protected, frozen-signature target: for `f : X ⟶ S` separated and quasi-compact, `F`
quasi-coherent, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, an isomorphism in the weak
existence form `Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` (under
`[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`), where `higherDirectImage f i F =
((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`. End-state: zero inline `sorry` in the cone, zero
project axioms, kernel-only axioms. Extraction from the Algebraic-Jacobian challenge; the
downstream Picard/Quot machinery is out of scope and was carved away.

The basis lemma is the linchpin that lifts narrowed standard-cover affine acyclicity to general-affine `H^q(U_σ,F)=0` (see Routes). |

DECIDED: narrow the non-protected signature to standard covers (downstream-safe *via the P5a basis lemma*), then build the localisation homotopy from scratch for `Scheme.Modules`. |

## Routes

### Route A — acyclic-resolution comparison (CHOSEN)
The augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` on `X` (`Cᵖ = ∏ (j_s)_*(F|_{U_s})` over
`(p+1)`-fold intersections) is (i) a resolution of `F` and (ii) termwise `(pushforward f)`-
acyclic, because each intersection is affine (relative Serre vanishing, P3). The abstract
homological-algebra theorem "a `G`-acyclic resolution computes `G.rightDerived`" (P4, built
by dimension-shifting from Mathlib's `InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`) then gives
`Hⁱ(f_* C•) = Hⁱ(CechComplex) ≅ (pushforward f).rightDerived i F` directly — ONE abstract
lemma, NO spectral sequences. This is the standard Cartan–Leray acyclic-cover proof of the
existence statement; it folds P3 in as its acyclicity input.

### Route B — two spectral sequences (REJECTED, fallback only)
The literal Stacks 02KE route: a Čech-to-derived spectral sequence plus the Leray spectral
sequence for `Scheme.Modules`. Rejected: both spectral sequences are absent from Mathlib
(multi-thousand-LOC to build), and the Leray degeneration additionally needs quasi-coherence
of `R^q f_* F` (`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images`, itself non-trivial via relative
Mayer–Vietoris). Strictly heavier than Route A for the same `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` goal. Kept on
record only as a fallback if Route A's abstract lemma proves unexpectedly hard.

### The P3-narrowing ↔ P5a-basis-lemma bridge (load-bearing)
Narrowing the non-protected `CechAcyclic.affine` signature to STANDARD covers is
downstream-safe **only** because the final assembly (P5b) does not apply `CechAcyclic.affine`
to the general finite-affine `𝒰` directly. Instead, the general-cover intersection vanishing
`H^q(U_σ, F) = 0` is obtained from narrowed standard-cover acyclicity through the basis lemma
`lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` (Stacks 01EO): standard affine covers `D(f_i)` form a basis,
and 01EO lifts Čech-acyclicity on a basis to sheaf-cohomology vanishing on every affine open.
Hence the basis lemma (scoped in P5a) is the linchpin connecting narrowed P3 to the general
`𝒰` in the frozen goal; "downstream-safe" is a *derived* claim contingent on building it.

## Open strategic questions

- P5a basis lemma `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` (Stacks 01EO): confirm whether Mathlib
  provides a faithful version for `Scheme.Modules` (→ `\mathlibok` anchor) or it must be built.
  This is the linchpin of the P3-narrowing safety argument (see Routes) — scope it (own P5a
  sub-goal). Its standalone statement is in Stacks `cohomology.tex`, not yet in `references/`.
- P3 statement gap: narrow `CechAcyclic.affine` to a standard-cover hypothesis (DECIDED, option
  a — downstream-safe via the P5a basis lemma). Open sub-question: the precise "standard affine
  cover" Lean type (non-trivial design); resolve in the P3 refactor before the effort-break.
- P3: exact Mathlib names for prime-local exactness (`algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local`
  analog, e.g. `Module.isZero` / localization-faithfulness) — verify before dispatching.
- P5b: termwise `(pushforward f)`-acyclicity reduces to affine Serre vanishing via Stacks
  `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing` after localising to affine `S` (resolved; no strategy change).
- P5a/P5b blueprint must be made Route-A-clean: the current sketches of
  `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` (Čech-to-derived SS) and `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`
  (relative Leray SS) invoke spectral sequences absent from Mathlib — contradicting Route A's

  before P5a can pass the gate as a parallel prover lane. First action once P4 closes.

      exact sequences `(Sₘ) : 0 → Zᵐ → Jᵐ → Z^{m+1} → 0` (`Z⁰ = A`, `Zᵐ = ker(Jᵐ→J^{m+1})`), with
      acyclic middle term. Pure abelian-category construction (kernels/images/corestrictions).
   2. **`CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedOneIsoCokerOfAcyclic`** (`lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker`,
      frontier-ready): for `0 → A → J → Z → 0` with `J` acyclic, `(R¹G)(A) ≅ coker(G(J)→G(Z))`.
      Reads the low-degree segment `G(J)→G(Z)→(R¹G)(A)→(R¹G)(J)=0` of the homology LES of `G(I_•)`
      (same engine as `rightDerivedShiftIsoOfSplitResolutionSES`, now via the horseshoe lift
      `ofShortExact` + `homology_exact₁/₂/₃` + acyclic vanishing). `\uses` injective_resolution_of_ses,
      homology_long_exact_sequence, right_derived_injective_resolution, right_derived_zero_iso_self.
   3. **`CategoryTheory.Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles`** (`lem:applied_cosyzygy_cycles`): `G` left-exact
      applied to `(Sₙ)` gives `G(Zⁿ) ≅ ker(G(Jⁿ)→G(J^{n+1}))` (the n-th cocycle object), `G(A) ≅ H⁰`.
      `\uses` cosyzygy_ses, right_derived_zero_iso_self.
   4. **`CategoryTheory.Functor.cohomologyAppliedResolutionIso`** (`lem:cohomology_of_applied_resolution`):
      `H⁰(G(J•)) ≅ G(A)`, `Hⁿ(G(J•)) ≅ coker(G(J^{n-1})→G(Zⁿ))` for `n ≥ 1`. `\uses` cosyzygy_ses,
      applied_cosyzygy_cycles, right_derived_zero_iso_self.
   5. **`CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`**
      (`lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived`, **TARGET 3** — the comparison theorem): for a
      `G`-acyclic resolution of `A`, `(Rⁿ G)(A) ≅ Hⁿ(G(J•))` for all `n ∈ ℕ`. Assembles: degree 0
      via (4); positive degrees by the staircase — iterate the dimension shift
      `rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic` (already proven, part 1) down to `(R¹G)(Z^{n-1})`, convert via
      (2), identify the cokernel with `Hⁿ` via (4). `\uses` def:right_acyclic, cosyzygy_ses,
      cohomology_of_applied_resolution, acyclic_dimension_shift, acyclic_one_iso_coker,
      right_derived_zero_iso_self, right_derived_vanishes_injective.
   Build axiom-clean, as far as possible (NO sorry pins). If a step genuinely blocks, hand off a
   precise decomposition (effort-breaker the still-hard sub-lemma) — but the chain is straight-line
   off already-proven decls, so it is expected to close P4 this iter. Verified-present infra:
   `rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic`, `rightDerivedShiftIsoOfSplitResolutionSES`,
   `InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`, `Functor.rightDerivedZeroIsoSelf`,
   `isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ`, `ShortComplex.ShortExact.homology_exact₁/₂/₃`/`.δ`/`.δIso`.
   Scaffold guidance: `.archon/analogies/p4-derived-les.md` + the `/- Planner strategy -/` block in
   the file. [prover-mode: mathlib-build]

Single ready lane this iter (P4). **Parallelism check (standing directive):** P4 is the only
gate-cleared, statement-sound lane and is a single bottom-up dependency chain in one file
(cosyzygy_ses ⇒ one_iso_coker ⇒ applied_cosyzygy_cycles ⇒ cohomology_of_applied_resolution ⇒
assembly), not splittable across provers without import coupling for marginal gain. P5a's frontier
nodes are gate-failed (their chapter is `partial`; two P5a/P5b sketches still route through
Mathlib-absent spectral sequences and need Route-A rewrites). P3 is blocked by its statement gap.
No second gate-cleared lane exists — honest serialization, full utilization of ready lanes.

## Deferred (not blocking the P4 lane)

- **P5a parallelisation (next front once P4 closes)**: `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` is
  `partial/partial`. The strategy-critic flagged P5a (augmented-Čech-resolution + `R^q(jₛ)_*=0`) as
  independent of P3/P4 and parallelisable. Blocker to making it a live lane: two P5a/P5b proof
  sketches (`lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` via a Čech-to-derived SS; `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`
  via the relative Leray SS) currently invoke spectral sequences ABSENT from Mathlib — they must be

  closes: dispatch a blueprint-writer to de-spectral-sequence those two sketches (Stacks 01EO basis
  argument + relative affine vanishing), then the clean P5a leaves (`cech_augmented_resolution`,
  `higher_direct_image_presheaf`) become parallel prover lanes alongside P3.
- **P3 statement gap** (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`): blueprint proves the STANDARD-cover case;
  `CechAcyclic.affine`'s Lean signature takes a general `X.OpenCover`. DECIDED: narrow the
  non-protected signature to a standard-cover hypothesis (downstream-safe via the P5a basis lemma —

  coverage-debt blocks (`quasiIso_τ₂`, `rightDerivedShiftIsoOfSplitResolutionSES`) were given

## Standing notes

#### HARD GATE detail — `lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker`

The statement `(R¹G)(A) ≅ coker(G(J)→G(Z))` for the short exact sequence `0→A→J→Z→0` with `J` right-`G`-acyclic is precisely and completely stated. The proof sketch reads off the low-degree segment `G(J)→G(Z)→(R¹G)(A)→(R¹G)(J)=0` of the long exact sequence produced by the horseshoe lift (`lem:injective_resolution_of_ses`) applied to the given SES, using `lem:homology_long_exact_sequence` to get the LES, `lem:right_derived_injective_resolution` to identify cohomology with derived functors, and acyclicity of `J` (`def:right_acyclic`) to kill the `(R¹G)(J)` term. The statement `(RⁿG)(A) ≅ Hⁿ(G(J•))` for all `n ∈ ℕ` under a `G`-acyclic resolution is complete and unambiguous. The proof sketch is fully decomposed:

- Degree 0: `H⁰(G(J•)) ≅ G(A)` via `lem:cohomology_of_applied_resolution`, transported to `(R⁰G)(A)` via `lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self`.

- The final reconciliation with injective resolutions is noted (injectives are acyclic by `lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective`).

### blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex
- **complete**: partial

#### Route-A contamination — per-lemma verdict

**`lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` — CONTAMINATED.**
The proof states: "the Čech-to-derived-functor spectral sequence for a cover `𝒰` of `B ∈ ℬ` has its higher rows killed by hypothesis (3) … so it collapses and the edge map … is an isomorphism." This invokes the Čech-to-derived spectral sequence, which is absent from Mathlib. The proof also contains the self-aware remark "The general basis-comparison criterion for sheaves of modules on a scheme is not yet available in Mathlib and is recorded here as a to-build dependency," confirming the present sketch is not prover-ready.

**Route-A rewrite feasibility**: Feasible via the acyclic-resolution / Mayer-Vietoris approach. The application of this lemma is always in the special case `ℬ` = affine opens of `U = Spec A`, `Cov` = standard covers of affine opens. In that case the Route-A argument is:

1. For an admissible standard cover `𝒰` of an affine `B ∈ ℬ`, the augmented Čech complex `0→F→C•` is exact by `lem:cech_augmented_resolution` (the same contracting-homotopy argument).
2. Each Čech term `Cᵖ = ∏ (jₛ)_*F|_{Uₛ}` (with `Uₛ` affine open) is acyclic for `Γ(B, -)`: for each affine `Uₛ`, the sheaf `(jₛ)_*F|_{Uₛ}` on `B` has `Hᵏ(B, (jₛ)_*F|_{Uₛ}) = Hᵏ(Uₛ, F|_{Uₛ}) = 0` for `k ≥ 1` — the first equality by the Leray/presheaf description (or, directly from the fact that `jₛ` is an open immersion and global sections factor), and the second by `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` applied to a standard cover of `Uₛ` (or directly by the same contracting homotopy at the module level). (Note: the second equality requires an inductive or alternative argument to avoid circularity — see below.)
3. By `lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived` applied with `G = Γ(B, -)`, `Hᵏ(B, F) ≅ Hᵏ(G(C•)) = Čech H^k = 0`.

The circularity concern (step 2 uses Serre vanishing on affines, which is what we want to prove) is resolved as follows: `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` gives `Čech-H^k = 0` for standard covers — this is purely an algebraic statement about complexes of localisations proved by a contracting homotopy. The transfer from Čech vanishing to sheaf-cohomology vanishing is exactly what `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` establishes. The argument becomes non-circular when written as: for `B = Spec A` with a STANDARD cover, apply `lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived` with `G = Γ(B, -)` directly to the augmented Čech complex of that standard cover, where acyclicity of each term is established by the SAME contracting homotopy argument (the prime-local homotopy of `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` proves `Γ(B, Cᵖ) → Γ(B, Cᵖ⁺¹)` is exact without any sheaf cohomology input). This bypasses the spectral sequence and uses only `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` + `lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived`. References needed: `references/stacks-cohomology.tex` (01EO, line 1696) for the statement, `references/stacks-coherent.tex` for the application context.

**`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp` — CONTAMINATED in part (2).**
Part (1) — `R^q j_*H = 0` for `q ≥ 1` — is Route-A-clean: uses `lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf` + `lem:affine_serre_vanishing` applied to affine `j^{-1}(V)`.
Part (2) — `R^k f_*(j_*H) ≅ R^k g_*H` — invokes the relative Leray spectral sequence (absent from Mathlib).

**Route-A rewrite feasibility**: Feasible using the P4 acyclic-resolution theorem:

1. Choose an injective resolution `H → I•` of `H` in `U.Modules`.
2. Apply `j_*` degreewise: `j_*H → j_*I•`.
